level_signals: Reports directly to GM of Creator
Responsible for building and managing a high-performing creator SLG marketing team
OTE range $200K-$275K consistent with VP-level at a growth-stage company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in creator marketing, growth marketing, or GTM roles with direct responsibility for acquisition at scale.
Background at a social media platform, creator economy company, or affiliate/influencer marketing business, with firsthand understanding of how creators evaluate and adopt monetization tools.
Proven track record of building and leading sales-led or sales-assisted growth programs that drove measurable creator or partner acquisition outcomes.
Experience partnering closely with Sales teams, including building GTM playbooks, creating sales enablement materials, and aligning marketing programs to pipeline goals.
Strong data orientation: you set up measurement frameworks, define success metrics, and use performance data to make fast decisions and reallocate investment.
Experience managing and growing a team, with a track record of building high-performing, collaborative functions from relatively early stages.
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English, including the ability to present strategy and results clearly to senior leadership.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity with affiliate marketing mechanics, creator monetization models, or marketplace dynamics is a meaningful advantage.

Experience with social platform creator programs (TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, Pinterest, or similar) and an understanding of what motivates creators to join and stay active in a monetization ecosystem.

scope.surface: Creator SLG (sales-led growth) — affiliate creator acquisition, activation, and retention across Later Influence and Mavely
scope.business_model: B2B2C — influencer/affiliate marketplace platform serving enterprise brands and creators
core_responsibilities: Own the creator SLG strategy end to end, defining the acquisition model, segmentation approach, and channel mix
Set the annual and quarterly GTM plan for creator SLG acquisition
Define the creator journey from initial outreach through activation
Build and run full-funnel creator acquisition campaigns spanning outbound, events, social, partnerships, and targeted paid
Lead creator segmentation and targeting with Data Science and Marketing Ops
Develop and launch creator-specific lifecycle programs
Serve as primary marketing partner to the Sales team covering creator acquisition
Build, manage, and develop a high-performing creator SLG marketing team
Define the measurement framework for creator SLG
Report on creator SLG performance to senior leadership

green_flags: Senior, high-visibility role reporting directly to GM of Creator
Clear 30/60/90-day and 12-month success milestones defined in the JD
Company has strong enterprise brand clients (Nike, Wayfair, Unilever, Southwest Airlines)
Explicit commitment to inclusion and equity in hiring
Encourages candidates who identify with any of the listed criteria to apply
red_flags: OTE structure not broken down (base vs. variable split unclear)
Equity not mentioned at all for a VP-level role
Posting first seen date not available — unclear how long this role has been open
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not stated
Team size and current state of the creator SLG function not disclosed — 'relatively early stages' suggests building from scratch which may be challenging

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, comp, and people management signals)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and success criteria)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD statement: 'benchmarked against market Total Cash Compensation for the geographic location')
ambiguities: Compensation is listed as OTE ($200K-$275K) but the base/variable split is not disclosed; the entire range could be interpreted as base-only or as full OTE.

JD footer tag is '#LI-Remote' and states 'fully remote candidates' are eligible for select positions, but offices are listed in Boston MA, Vancouver BC, Chicago IL, and Vancouver WA — unclear whether this specific role requires any in-office presence.
Equity is not mentioned; unusual for a VP-level role — may be an omission or intentional.
